raw_text:
Prism Labs

Building intuitive interfaces that connect people to information.

Now Hiring: Staff UX Designer

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly) · Full-time · $220,000 - $290,000 per year

W

e're seeking an experienced Staff UX Designer to lead design strategy and technical execution across our

product ecosystem. In this role, you'll mentor junior designers, establish design systems and standards, drive

cross-functional collaboration with engineering and product leadership, and own the user experience for multiple

high-impact initiatives. This is a technical leadership position for someone with 8+ years of proven design

excellence and a demonstrated ability to influence product direction at scale.

A B O U T U S

Prism Labs is a venture-backed design and technology company focused on creating innovative digital products

for enterprise and consumer markets. Founded in 2018, we specialize in developing accessible, data-driven user

experiences that help teams collaborate more effectively. Our platform is used by over 50,000 professionals across

healthcare, finance, and education sectors.

Y O U R R O L E

Lead the evolution and maintenance of our comprehensive design system, ensuring consistency, accessibility

compliance (WCAG AA/AAA standards), and scalability across all product surfaces using Figma as the single
source of truth

Conduct and synthesize user research using mixed-methods approaches (interviews, usability testing, analytics)

to inform strategic product decisions and validate design hypotheses at the staff level

Mentor and develop junior and mid-level UX designers, providing feedback, career guidance, and technical

skill-building in prototyping tools, design systems, and accessibility best practices

Establish design standards, component libraries, and documentation practices that empower cross-functional

teams to ship high-quality experiences independently

Partner with engineering and product leaders to advocate for user-centered design in technical architecture

decisions and prioritize accessibility as a core feature, not an afterthought

Prototype and validate complex interactions using Figma prototyping, Axure RP, or similar tools to

communicate designs with precision and test edge cases before development

Lead accessibility audits using tools like WAVE and Axe, working with developers to resolve WCAG

violations and establish accessibility-first design practices across the organization

 Champion design thinking and user-centered methodologies across product and engineering teams through

workshops, case studies, and cross-functional leadership

Contribute to hiring and evaluation of design talent, setting technical bar and cultural fit expectations for the
team

R E Q U I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

8+ years of professional UX/UI design experience in digital product design, with at least 3 years in a senior or

lead role driving design systems, mentorship, or cross-team initiatives

Expert-level proficiency with Figma, including components, variables, prototyping, design systems, and team

collaboration workflows

Demonstrated expertise in user research methodologies (qualitative and quantitative) and ability to synthesize

insights into actionable design direction

Strong portfolio showcasing complex, scalable design systems and accessibility-compliant interfaces; evidence

of designing for diverse user needs and contexts

Proven track record of successfully mentoring designers and raising the design craft within organizations;
comfort with giving and receiving critical feedback

Deep knowledge of accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA minimum) and hands-on experience auditing and

improving accessible design using tools like WAVE, Axe, or similar

Excellent communication and presentation skills; ability to influence stakeholders and articulate design

rationale to both technical and non-technical audiences

D E S I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

Experience designing for or managing products in regulated industries (healthcare, finance) with complex
compliance requirements

Proficiency in one or more prototyping tools beyond Figma (Axure RP, Framer, or similar) to demonstrate

advanced interaction design capability

Hands-on experience with front-end code (HTML, CSS, or React) or demonstrated collaboration patterns with

engineers in design-to-dev handoff workflows

Experience with design tokens, CSS-in-JS, or other design-engineering integration frameworks

Published writing, speaking, or workshop facilitation on design systems, accessibility, or UX strategy in

professional contexts

T O O L S  O F  T H E T R A D E

Figma, Design Systems & Component Libraries, User Research & Analytics, Prototyping (Axure RP, interactive

Figma prototypes), Accessibility Auditing (WAVE, Axe, Lighthouse), WCAG 2.1 Compliance Standards, Design
Tokens & CSS-in-JS Integration, Usability Testing Platforms (UserTesting, Maze), Interaction Design &

Microinteractions, Design Documentation & Knowledge Management (Notion, Confluence)

 W E O F F E R

Competitive equity package and annual bonus (15% target)

Medical, dental, and vision insurance with 90% employer coverage for employee plans

Flexible work-from-home policy with optional San Francisco office space

Professional development budget ($5,000 annually) for courses, conferences, and design tools

$10,000 annual wellness stipend (gym membership, mental health, wellness retreats)

Generous PTO policy: 25 days paid time off plus 10 company holidays

Parental leave (16 weeks paid for primary caregiver, 12 weeks for secondary)

Design tool subscriptions (Figma, Adobe Creative Suite, Axure, prototyping tools) provided by company

T O A P P LY

Submit your application at careers.prismlabs.io/staff-ux-designer. Include your resume, a portfolio link (Figma
showcase or case study website preferred), and a 1-2 page cover letter describing your approach to design systems

or mentorship. Candidates will be screened for portfolio quality and staff-level design thinking. We review

applications on a rolling basis.
nice_to_have: Medical, dental, and vision insurance with 90% employer coverage for employee plans
Flexible work-from-home policy with optional San Francisco office space
Professional development budget ($5,000 annually) for courses, conferences, and design tools
$10,000 annual wellness stipend (gym membership, mental health, wellness retreats)
Generous PTO policy: 25 days paid time off plus 10 company holidays
Parental leave (16 weeks paid for primary caregiver, 12 weeks for secondary)
Design tool subscriptions (Figma, Adobe Creative Suite, Axure, prototyping tools) provided by company
